arts.ttl: Man Utd transfer new: Three clubs enter Rashford race
arts.sum: Manchester United forward Marcus Rashford has emerged as a key target for Saudi Arabian clubs Al-Ahli, Al-Ittihad, and Al-Qadsiah. And now the outlet has said that all three Saudi Pro League clubs are keen to sign Rashford, whether in January or during the summer window.

arts.ttl: Man Utd star shares first impressions of Leny Yoro after finally playing together
arts.sum: Leny Yoro was one of Manchester United's major signings back in the summer but injury put his debut on hold with Matthijs de Ligt now getting to see his class up close

arts.ttl: Team News: Manchester United vs. Bournemouth injury, suspension list, predicted XIs
arts.sum: Sports Mole rounds up all of the latest injury and suspension news ahead of Sunday's Premier League clash between Manchester United and Bournemouth.

arts.ttl: Graeme Souness slams Marcus Rashford for 'quietly accepting' Man Utd transfer exit
arts.sum: Liverpool legend Graeme Souness has laid into Marcus Rashford, claiming his career will go "downhill" if he does end up leaving Manchester United in the near future

arts.ttl: Marcus Rashford swap deal option as £64m Man United transfer target becomes available
arts.sum: Manchester United and Paris Saint-Germain could entertain a potential swap deal during the January transfer window with Randal Kolo Muani now available

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta's four-word comment speaks volumes after Marcus Rashford's Man Utd exit door opens
arts.sum: Marcus Rashford is expected to leave Manchester United in January after announcing his intention to leave his boyhood club, with Arsenal a possible destination for the forward

arts.ttl: Ruben Amorim identifies three areas he's improved Man Utd since replacing Erik ten Hag
arts.sum: Ruben Amorim arrived at Manchester United from Sporting Lisbon looking to overhaul the squad and their playing style to give the team an identity, but has endured a mixed start to his reign with four wins, three defeats and one draw.

arts.ttl: Manager the one pushing club to sign Manchester United man – ‘Sponsored’ move, ‘convinced’ it can work
arts.sum: Juventus manager Thiago Motta is the one pushing for the club to sign Joshua Zirkzee from Manchester United in the January transfer window. That’s according to Sportmediaset, relayed by Tutto Juve, who say Motta is convinced that his former striker can improve his side in the second half of the season. The newspaper reports that The post Manager the one pushing club to sign Manchester United man – ‘Sponsored’ move, ‘convinced’ it can work appeared first on Sport Witness.

arts.ttl: Jadon Sancho makes his feelings clear on Marcus Rashford and Man Utd saga
arts.sum: Marcus Rashord and Manchester United appear set to part ways in January or beyond after the forward revealed earlier this week he believes he should leave Old Trafford

arts.ttl: Amad Diallo becomes jewel in crown for Amorim’s Manchester United
arts.sum: Winger has jumped to the top of the pecking order of wide men since Portuguese took the reins at Old Trafford Most of the noise around those in red at the Etihad Stadium last Sunday related to the dropping of two wingers until Amad Diallo intercepted Matheus Nunes’s woeful back-pass and won a penalty before scoring a last-minute winner. The Ivorian was Manchester United’s main threat in a mediocre derby as his flourishing under Ruben Amorim continued, while others drifted into the shadows. As Marcus Rashford and Alejandro Garnacho endured a watching brief after being stood down, putting them below Antony in the pecking order, Diallo shone. The new head coach is constantly tinkering and rotating personnel as he searches for the best players for individual roles but Diallo is making himself almost undroppable and will get another start against Bournemouth on Sunday as he aims to add to his two league goals and six assists this season.
